conclusion: Sosin gives the investor version of Carvana's near-death and recovery. The thesis is not just online used cars; it is a national logistics, reconditioning, retail, and financing system that took more than a decade and roughly $10B to build.
background: Cliff Sosin founded CAS Investment Partners after Houlihan Lokey, Silver Point, and UBS. He built the firm around concentrated, long-duration ownership of businesses through public stocks, even though that approach is hard to market because feedback loops are noisy and multi-year. Carvana is the centerpiece. Sosin first studied it in 2018, watched the company scale rapidly, then defended the position through a 99% drawdown when units, losses, leverage, and sentiment all moved against him.
important_facts: Sosin launched with $5.2M: about $2M from himself, $2M from his mother, and $1M from a friend. The firm later ran roughly $1.5-2B across vehicles. At the low point, Sosin says he had expected 800,000 cars but the company was tracking closer to 300,000, expected profit but was tracking toward a roughly $2B loss, and the stock was down 99%. Sosin first studied it in 2018, watched the company scale rapidly, then defended the position through a 99% drawdown when units, losses, leverage, and sentiment all moved against him.

Carvana was founded in 2013. At the low point, Sosin says he had expected 800,000 cars but the company was tracking closer to 300,000, expected profit but was tracking toward a roughly $2B loss, and the stock was down 99%. The thesis is not just online used cars; it is a national logistics, reconditioning, retail, and financing system that took more than a decade and roughly $10B to build.

Carvana's large inspection and reconditioning centers can handle up to about 40,000 cars per year and hold 6,000-8,000 cars. Sosin says Carvana has spent more than 10 years and about $10B building the system. Investment implications in the summary are: CVNA (Long) — Sosin argues national inventory, lower reconditioning/logistics cost, 10.5% EBITDA margins rising toward 13-14%, and 45-50% recent growth support the recovery.

mental_model.name: Ugly Path, Superior System
mental_model.components: Carvana's moat is the whole system, not the website. The experience requires national inventory, local hubs, nine-car haulers, large IRCs, customer financing, trust, reconditioning speed, and edge-case handling. Competitors can imitate pieces, but the integrated network took years and billions to assemble. The 2022 crisis was partly self-inflicted and partly survivable. Carvana scaled for a much larger 2022 after selling roughly 425,000 cars in 2021, then demand slowed, losses ballooned, ADESA added debt, and the stock fell 99%. Sosin's claim is that the business is now more profitable and back on track. Long-only concentration is hard because it is anti-marketing. He buys a small number of stocks as business ownership and waits through noisy, multi-year feedback loops. That does not produce the clean, short-cycle proof points committees like, so it maximizes returns more than marketability.
mental_model.application: Sosin gives the investor version of Carvana's near-death and recovery. The thesis is not just online used cars; it is a national logistics, reconditioning, retail, and financing system that took more than a decade and roughly $10B to build. top_investment_implications.thesis: Sosin argues national inventory, lower reconditioning/logistics cost, 10.5% EBITDA margins rising toward 13-14%, and 45-50% recent growth support the recovery
Carvana's national pooled inventory and lower online operating model pressure CarMax's store-centric used-car retail economics
If used cars behave more like books, scaled online inventory and trust can shift conversion and pricing away from local dealership models

golden_quotes: "At the low point of the thesis, Sosin was staring at a stock that was down 99% while the business looked dramatically worse than he had expected."
"Patrick summarizes Sosin's process almost comically: Cliff picks stocks by thinking really hard for a very long time."
"Sosin's key clarification is that Carvana is not merely a website; it is an online retailer of used cars backed by a national operating system."

events.event: Sosin started his investment firm after UBS
Sosin first encountered Carvana and began studying the system
Carvana sold roughly 425,000 cars and planned for much larger 2022 growth
Demand slowed, losses expanded, ADESA increased debt, and the stock fell about 99%
Sosin argues the company is more profitable, growing again, and mostly recovered in the market
